docs.title: Docs Stack – Material
docs.text:
Status: Live (v1.0)

A professional documentation system built with MkDocs and Material for MkDocs.

docs.title: What this project provides
docs.text:
• Production-grade documentation structure
  • Clean navigation and search
  • Version-controlled content

docs.title: Who should use this
docs.text:
• Backend developers
  • API engineers
  • Open-source contributors

docs.title: Featured Projects
docs.text:
Explore the documentation for my key projects:

  • Portfolio React: Modern developer portfolio with admin dashboard.
  • Noirel Ecommerce: Premium e-commerce application.
  • Time Lens Python: Mindset tool to reframe time perception.
  • Salary Reality Checker: AI-powered salary analytics platform.
  • Todo Redux Advanced: Advanced Todo app with Redux Toolkit.
  • Fullstack Blog JWT: Secure full-stack blog with Docker.
  • Student Management: Comprehensive Django-based student system.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: The projects in this portfolio primarily adhere to modern web development architectures, ensuring scalability, maintainability, and performance.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• Component-Based: Built mainly with React.js, emphasizing reusable UI components.
  • State Management: Utilizing Context API, Redux Toolkit, or Zustand depending on complexity.
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS is the standard for responsive and utility-first styling.

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• REST API: Most backends (Django, FastAPI, Node.js) expose RESTful endpoints.
  • MVC / MVT: Django projects follow the Model-View-Template pattern (similar to MVC).
  • Microservices vs Monolith: Smaller tools (Time Lens) are microservices/functions, while larger platforms (Student Management, Noirel) are modular monoliths.

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
• Relational: PostgreSQL is the preferred choice for complex data relations (Salary Checker, Student Management).
  • NoSQL: MongoDB or JSON Server is used for lighter or rapid-prototyping projects (Portfolio).

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
• Frontend: Vercel / Netlify.
  • Backend: Render / Railway / Heroku.
  • Containerization: Docker is used to standardize runtime environments.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Most projects in this portfolio use environment variables for configuration to ensure security and flexibility.

docs.title: Common Environment Variables
docs.text: While each project has unique needs, you will commonly encounter these:

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
• DATABASE_URL: Connection string for PostgreSQL or MongoDB.
  • DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD: Individual connection parameters.

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• SECRET_KEY: Used for cryptographic signing (Django/Flask).
  • JWT_SECRET: Secret key for signing JSON Web Tokens.
  • ADMIN_PASSWORD: Default password for initial admin accounts.

docs.title: External Services
docs.text:
• VITE_API_URL: Backend API URL for frontend applications.
  • STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / RAZORPAY_KEY_ID: Payment gateway credentials.
  • EMAIL_HOST_USER, EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD: SMTP credentials for sending emails.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Copy the example env file (often named .env.example).
  2. Rename it to .env.
  3. Fill in your specific values.

[!WARNING] Never commit your .env file to version control. It is excluded by default in .gitignore.

docs.title: Conventions
docs.text: To maintain consistency across projects, the following conventions are generally observed.

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Formatting: Prettier for JavaScript/React; Black for Python.
  • Linting: ESLint for JS; Flake8/Pylint for Python.

docs.title: Commit Messages
docs.text:
We follow the Conventional Commits specification:

  • feat: ... for new features.
  • fix: ... for bug fixes.
  • docs: ... for documentation updates.
  • style: ... for formatting changes.
  • refactor: ... for code refactoring.

docs.title: Branching
docs.text:
• main / master: Production-ready code.
  • dev: Development branch (if applicable).
  • feature/<name>: New feature development.
  • bugfix/<name>: Bug fixes.

docs.title: File Naming
docs.text:
• React Components: PascalCase (e.g., Button.tsx, Navbar.jsx).
  • JS/TS Utilities: camelCase (e.g., formatDate.ts).
  • Python Modules: snake_case (e.g., utils.py, models.py).

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to the documentation for Ashif's Project Portfolio. This collection showcases a diverse range of open-source projects, from full-stack web applications to utility tools and data analysis platforms.

docs.title: What's Included?
docs.text:
This site mainly documents the following key projects:

  • Portfolio React: A personal developer portfolio with an admin dashboard.
  • Noirel Ecommerce: A high-end e-commerce experience.
  • Salary Checker: AI-driven salary insights and predictions.
  • Time Lens: A psychological tool for time perception.

Each project documentation includes its specific features, tech stack, and installation guides.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Most projects in this portfolio rely on the following technologies. Ensure you have them installed before running them locally:

  • Node.js (v18+ recommended) for React/Next.js projects.
  • Python (v3.10+) for Django/FastAPI backends.
  • Docker for containerized applications.
  • Git for version control.

docs.title: Installation Overview
docs.text: Since this documentation covers multiple independent projects, installation steps vary by repository. However, most follow a standard pattern.

docs.title: General Installation Steps
docs.text:
1. Clone the Repository: bash git clone https://github.com/ashif-ek/<repo-name>.git cd <repo-name>

  2. Install Dependencies:

    • For JavaScript/Video projects: bash npm install # or yarn install
    • For Python projects: bash python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate # Windows: venv\Scripts\activate pip install -r requirements.txt

  3. Environment Configuration:

    • Most projects require a .env file. Check the specific project documentation or README.md for required variables.

  4. Run the Application:

    • JS: npm run dev
    • Python (Django): python manage.py runserver
    • Python (FastAPI): uvicorn main:app --reload

docs.title: Project-Specific Guides
docs.text:
For detailed instructions, refer to the specific project pages:

  • Portfolio React Installation
  • Noirel Ecommerce Installation
  • Salary Checker Setup
  • Time Lens Setup

docs.title: Authentication API
docs.text: Secure access handling across applications.

docs.title: JWT Implementation
docs.text: Projects like Fullstack Blog and Student Management use JSON Web Tokens (JWT).

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
1. Login: POST /api/token/
    • Payload: {username, password}
    • Response: {access, refresh}
  2. Access Protected Routes:
    • Header: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
  3. Refresh Token: POST /api/token/refresh/
    • Payload: {refresh}

docs.title: Admin Access
docs.text: Admin panels are protected via session auth or specific internal roles. * Django Admin: /admin/ (Standard Django auth). * Portfolio Admin: Custom implementation.

docs.title: Database Schema & API
docs.text: How data is structured and accessed.

docs.title: User Model (Common)
docs.text:
• id: UUID or Integer.
  • username: String, unique.
  • email: String, unique.
  • is_active: Boolean.

docs.title: Domain Specific
docs.text:
• Ecommerce: Product, Order, CartItem.
  • Education: Student, Course, Enrollment.
  • Salary: SalaryData (role, experience, city, amount).

docs.title: ORM Interaction
docs.text:
• Django ORM: Model.objects.filter(...)
  • SQLAlchemy: session.query(Model).filter(...)
  • Mongoose/JSON: Direct object manipulation.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: Standardized error responses.

docs.title: HTTP Status Codes
docs.text:
• 200 OK: Success.
  • 201 Created: Resource successfully created.
  • 400 Bad Request: Validation error.
  • 401 Unauthorized: Missing or invalid token.
  • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions.
  • 404 Not Found: Resource does not exist.
  • 500 Server Error: Internal application failure.

docs.title: Error Response Format
docs.text:
{
  "status": "error",
  "code": 400,
  "message": "Invalid password format",
  "details": {
    "field": "password",
    "issue": "Must contain at least one number"
  }
}

docs.title: API Documentation
docs.text: This section provides an overview of the APIs exposed by the various backend services.

docs.title: Implementation Details
docs.text: Most backends in this portfolio are self-documenting: * FastAPI & Django (DRF): Automatically generate Swagger/OpenAPI documentation. * Visit /docs or /redoc on the running backend server to explore endpoints interactively.

docs.title: Salary Checker
docs.text:
• GET /salary/insights: Retrieve percentile data.
  • POST /salary/predict: ML-based salary prediction.

docs.title: Portfolio Backend
docs.text:
• GET /projects: List all projects.
  • POST /messages: Submit contact form data.

docs.title: Noirel Ecommerce
docs.text:
• GET /products: Fetch catalog.
  • POST /cart: Manage shopping cart.
  • POST /orders: Place a new order.

Refer to the individual project repositories for the most up-to-date API contracts.

docs.title: Customization
docs.text: How to modify and extend the projects.

docs.title: Tailwind CSS Theme
docs.text: Most styled projects use tailwind.config.js. You can customize: * Colors: Modify theme.extend.colors to change the palette. * Fonts: Update fontFamily settings.

docs.title: React Components
docs.text: UI components are typically found in src/components. * Reusability: Components are built to be generic. * Props: Check the interface definitions (TypeScript) or PropTypes (JS) to understand available options.

docs.title: Backend Logic
docs.text:
• Django: Apps are modular. To add specific business logic, create a new app: python manage.py startapp <name>.
  • FastAPI: Add new routers in the api/ directory and include them in main.py.

docs.title: Deployment Strategies
docs.text: This guide outlines how to deploy the various applications in this portfolio.

docs.title: Frontend Deployment (Vercel)
docs.text:
Most React/Next.js frontends are optimized for Vercel.

  1. Connect GitHub: detailed instructions...
  2. Import Project: Select the repository.
  3. Build Settings:
    • Framework Preset: Vite or Next.js
    • Build Command: npm run build
    • Output Directory: dist or .next
  4. Environment Variables: copy from your local .env.

docs.title: Backend Deployment (Render)
docs.text:
Render is the preferred choice for Python and Node.js backends.

  1. Create Web Service.
  2. Connect Repo.
  3. Runtime: Python 3 or Node.
  4. Build Command:
    • Python: pip install -r requirements.txt
    • Node: npm install
  5. Start Command:
    • FastAPI: uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT
    • Django: gunicorn core.wsgi:application

docs.title: Docker Deployment
docs.text:
For containerized apps (like fullstack-blog-jwt-docker), use the Dockerfile:

docker build -t my-app .
docker run -p 8000:8000 my-app

docs.title: Local Development Guide
docs.text: This guide covers the general workflow for working with the projects locally.

docs.title: Prerequisite Tools
docs.text: Ensure you have the following installed: * Git * Node.js (LTS version) * Python (3.10+) * VS Code (Recommended IDE)

docs.title: Setting Up a Project
docs.text:
1. Clone: git clone <url>
  2. Env Vars: Create .env from .env.example.
  3. Dependencies: npm install or pip install -r requirements.txt.

docs.title: Running Servers
docs.text:
• Frontend: Usually runs on http://localhost:5173 (Vite) or 3000 (Next.js).
  • Backend: Usually runs on http://localhost:8000 (Django/FastAPI) or 5000 (Node/Express).

docs.title: Debugging
docs.text:
• Frontend: Use React Developer Tools.
  • Backend: Check terminal logs for stack traces. Use pdb for Python debugging if needed.

docs.title: Fullstack Blog JWT Docker
docs.text: A robust, production-ready Blog Application that bridges a high-performance Django backend with a dynamic React frontend. This project demonstrates a complete full-stack workflow, featuring secure authentication, containerized deployment, and a modern responsive UI.

docs.title: 🚀 Key Features
docs.text:
• Secure Authentication: Robust implementation of JWT (JSON Web Tokens) with access and refresh token rotation.
  • Containerization: Fully Dockerized environment using docker-compose for seamless orchestration of Backend (Django), Frontend (Vite), and Database (PostgreSQL).
  • Modern Frontend: Built with React 18 and Vite for lightning-fast HMR and build performance.
  • Responsive UI: Styled with Tailwind CSS for a mobile-first, clean aesthetic.
  • RESTful API: Well-structured API endpoints built with Django REST Framework (DRF).
  • CRUD Operations: Complete management of Blog Posts, including creation, editing, deleting, and viewing.

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• Framework: Django 4+ & Django REST Framework
  • Authentication: Simple JWT
  • Database: PostgreSQL
  • Containerization: Docker & Docker Compose

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• Framework: React 18
  • Build Tool: Vite
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS
  • State Management: React Context / Hooks
  • HTTP Client: Axios with Interceptors (for auto-token refresh)

docs.title: Option 1: Docker (Recommended)
docs.text:
Run the entire stack with a single command.

  1. Clone the repository: bash git clone https://github.com/ashif-ek/fullstack-blog-jwt-docker.git cd fullstack-blog-jwt-docker

  2. Start Services: bash docker-compose up --build

    • Frontend: http://localhost:5173
    • Backend API: http://localhost:8000/api
    • Admin Panel: http://localhost:8000/admin

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
1. Navigate to backend/: bash cd backend python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate # Windows: venv\Scripts\activate pip install -r requirements.txt
  2. Run Migrations & Server: bash python manage.py migrate python manage.py runserver

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
1. Navigate to frontend/: bash cd frontend npm install
  2. Start Dev Server: bash npm run dev

docs.title: 🔐 API Endpoints
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description POST /api/token/ Obtain Access & Refresh Tokens POST /api/token/refresh/ Refresh Access Token GET /api/posts/ List all blog posts POST /api/posts/ Create a new post (Auth required) GET /api/posts/<id>/ Retrieve a single post PUT /api/posts/<id>/ Update a post (Owner only)

docs.title: 🏗️ Docker Architecture
docs.text:
The docker-compose.yml orchestrates three main services: 1. db: PostgreSQL database. 2. backend: Django API (depends on db). 3. frontend: React App (depends on backend).

This ensures that the database is ready before the backend starts, and the backend is up before the frontend tries to connect.

View on GitHub

docs.title: NOIRÉL | Premium E-Commerce Experience
docs.text: NOIRÉL is a modern, high-performance e-commerce application built to deliver a seamless and premium shopping experience. It features a responsive design, optimistic UI updates for instant feedback, and a robust state management system.

docs.title: 🚀 Key Features
docs.text:
• Premium UI/UX: Minimalist, high-end aesthetic with smooth transitions and responsive layouts.
  • Optimized Performance:
    • Lazy Loading: Route-based code splitting for fast initial load times.
    • Optimistic UI: Instant updates for Cart and Wishlist actions with automatic background synchronization.
  • Robust State Management: Scalable Context API implementation using useReducer for predictable state transitions.
  • Secure Authentication: User registration and login with persistent sessions.
  • Reliability: Global Error Boundaries to gracefully handle runtime crashes.
  • Checkout & Payment: Integrated Razorpay payment gateway with dual-currency support (USD/INR).

docs.title: 🛠️ Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Frontend: React 19, Vite, TailwindCSS
  • State Management: React Context API + useReducer
  • Routing: React Router DOM v7
  • Icons & Components: Lucide React, React Icons
  • Payment: Razorpay
  • Quality: ESLint, Prettier

docs.title: 📦 Installation & Setup
docs.text:
Clone the repository:

git clone https://github.com/ashif-ek/noirel-ecommerce.git
cd noirel

Install Dependencies:

npm install

Environment Configuration: Create a .env file in the root directory (optional but recommended for API keys):

VITE_API_URL=https://noirel-server.onrender.com

Run Development Server:

npm run dev

Build for Production:

npm run build

docs.title: State Management
docs.text: We use a centralized Context architecture optimized for performance: * CartContext: Handles add/remove/update operations with optimistic updates and automatic rollback on error. * OrderContext: Manages checkout flows, exchange rate caching, and payment processing.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
• Global Error Boundary: Catches unhandled exceptions in the component tree to prevent white-screen crashes.
  • Graceful Fallbacks: UI components handle loading and error states locally.

docs.title: 🤝 Contributing
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository.
  2. Create your feature branch (git checkout -b feature/AmazingFeature).
  3. Commit your changes (git commit -m 'Add some AmazingFeature').
  4. Push to the branch (git push origin feature/AmazingFeature).
  5. Open a Pull Request.

View on GitHub

docs.title: Personal Portfolio Website
docs.text: A modern, responsive, and feature-rich personal portfolio website built with React, Vite, and Node.js. Designed to showcase projects, skills, and professional experience with a premium aesthetic and seamless user experience.

docs.title: 🚀 Features
docs.text:
• Modern UI/UX: Built with Tailwind CSS v4 and Framer Motion for smooth animations and glassmorphism effects.
  • Theme Support: Fully integrated Dark and Light modes with persistent state.
  • Admin Dashboard: Secure admin panel to manage content, view stats, and upload files.
  • Dynamic Content: Backend powered by JSON Server with custom middleware for easy content management.
  • Image Uploads: Custom file upload functionality with Multer.
  • Contact Integration: Formspree integration for contact form submissions.
  • Responsive Design: Fully responsive layout optimized for Mobile, Tablet, and Desktop.
  • PWA Ready: Configured with Vite PWA plugin for installable app-like experience.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• Framework: React 18
  • Build Tool: Vite
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS
  • Animations: Framer Motion
  • Icons: Lucide React, React Icons
  • Charts: Recharts
  • Routing: React Router

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• Runtime: Node.js
  • Server: JSON Server (Custom Interface)
  • File Handling: Multer
  • Database: JSON Server

docs.title: 📦 Installation
docs.text:
Clone the repository:

git clone https://github.com/ashif-ek/portfolio-react.git
cd portfolio

docs.title: Frontend Setup
docs.text:
Navigate to the frontend directory:

cd my-app

Install dependencies:

npm install

Start the development server:

npm run dev

docs.title: Backend Setup
docs.text:
Navigate to the server directory:

cd server

Install dependencies:

npm install

Configure environment variables: Create a .env file in the server directory with the following:

PORT=5000
ADMIN_USERNAME=admin
ADMIN_PASSWORD=your_secure_password

Start the server:

npm start

docs.title: Frontend (Vercel)
docs.text: The frontend is optimized for deployment on Vercel. Connect your repository and set the root directory to my-app.

docs.title: Backend (Render)
docs.text:
The backend is set up for Render. Ensure you set the Root Directory to server and add the environment variables in the Render dashboard.

View on GitHub

docs.title: Salary Reality Checker – AI-Powered Salary Insights
docs.text:
A FAANG-style salary analytics platform that calculates market percentiles, predicts salaries using machine learning, and auto-corrects user input using fuzzy matching.

Built with: * FastAPI * PostgreSQL * SQLAlchemy * scikit-learn ML * React + Vite + TailwindCSS * Recharts + Framer Motion

docs.title: 🔹 Smart Percentile Engine
docs.text: Calculates: * P10 * P25 * P50 (Median) * P75 * P90

docs.title: 🔹 Fuzzy Matching (AI Auto-Correction)
docs.text: Even if user types: py devloper, sofware eng, hyd, blr → App finds the closest valid match.

docs.title: 🔹 ML Salary Prediction
docs.text: If database has no entry: * Trains a Linear Regression model. * Predicts salary based on experience trends.

docs.title: 🔹 Admin Tools
docs.text:
• Upload CSV dataset.
  • Bulk insert into PostgreSQL.

docs.title: 🔹 Modern UI (React)
docs.text:
• Annual / Monthly toggle.
  • Animated charts.
  • Shimmer loading state.
  • Auto-suggest dropdown.
  • Dark & Light mode.

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• FastAPI
  • SQLAlchemy
  • PostgreSQL
  • scikit-learn
  • RapidFuzz

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• React (Vite)
  • TailwindCSS
  • Recharts
  • Framer Motion

docs.title: 📡 API Endpoints
docs.text:
• Add Salary POST /salary/add
  • Bulk Upload Dataset POST /salary/bulk
  • Get Salary Insights (Percentiles + ML) GET /salary/insights?job_role=&city=&experience=
  • Predict Salary GET /salary/predict?job_role=&city=&experience=

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
cd backend
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn app.main:app --reload

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev

docs.title: 📊 Demo Preview
docs.text:
• Percentile charts
  • Salary comparison
  • Fuzzy auto-correct input fields
  • ML-powered predictions
  • Elegant FAANG-grade UI

docs.title: Student Management Django Task
docs.text:
A complete student management platform built with Django, featuring authentication, role-based dashboards, course management, email notifications. Includes custom user models (Admin/Student), comprehensive CRUD operations, and a clean MVT architecture.

View on GitHub

docs.title: Time Lens — A New Way to See Time
docs.text:
Time Lens is a mindset tool that transforms how we perceive time. Instead of thinking in hours and minutes, this tool converts real time into a powerful life-model:

  • 1 real day = 1 life-year
  • 1 hour = 1 life-month
  • 1 minute = 1 life-day
  • 1 second = 1 life-hour

The goal is simple: Help you feel the true weight of time so every minute becomes intentional.

Live Demo

docs.title: 🎯 Why This Exists
docs.text:
People say “value your time,” but few explain how to actually value a minute. Time Lens reframes every real minute into a life-day—making even micro-actions meaningful.

Suddenly: * Wasting a minute feels like wasting a day. * Wasting an hour feels like losing a month. * Using a few minutes wisely feels like real progress.

docs.title: What Time Lens Gives You
docs.text:
For any real duration, the tool returns: * Life-days, life-months, and life-years. * A deep insight. * A meaningful action. * A psychological explanation. * A warning if wasted. * A description of who you become if you use it well.

Built with a custom advanced suggestion engine.

docs.title: 🛠️ Tech Stack
docs.text:
• Python
  • FastAPI
  • Uvicorn
  • HTML + CSS
  • JavaScript
  • Deployed on Vercel

docs.title: 📦 Run Locally

Visit: http://127.0.0.1:8000

docs.title: Todo Redux Advanced
docs.text:
Todo App is a production-ready Todo application built with React, Vite, and Redux Toolkit. It uses async thunks, debounced search, and throttled actions for efficient state and API management. Features include responsive UI, persistent state, and optimized performance.

docs.title: CLI Commands
docs.text: Reference for command-line tools used in this stack.

docs.title: JavaScript / Node
docs.text:
• npm install: Install dependencies.
  • npm run dev: Start dev server.
  • npm run build: Build for production.
  • npm run lint: Check code quality.

docs.title: Python / Django
docs.text:
• python manage.py runserver: Start dev server.
  • python manage.py makemigrations: Create DB migrations.
  • python manage.py migrate: Apply DB migrations.
  • python manage.py createsuperuser: Create admin account.

docs.title: Python / FastAPI
docs.text: • uvicorn main:app --reload: Start server with hot reload.

docs.title: Configuration Options
docs.text:
Detailed reference for environment variables.

Variable Description Required Default DEBUG Enable debug mode No False SECRET_KEY App secret for signing Yes - DATABASE_URL DB Connection string Yes - ALLOWED_HOSTS Comma-separated domains Yes * CORS_ORIGIN Frontend URL for CORS Yes - PORT Server port No 8000

See Configuration for setup instructions.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Terms used across the project documentation.

  • JWT (JSON Web Token): Compact URL-safe means of representing claims to be transferred between two parties.
  • MVT (Model-View-Template): Architectural pattern used by Django.
  • SPA (Single Page Application): Web app that interacts with the web browser by dynamically rewriting the current web page with new data from the web server.
  • ORM (Object-Relational Mapping): Technique for converting data between incompatible type systems using object-oriented programming languages.
  • Hook: Special function in React that lets you “hook into” React features.
